NA28.name: Nestle-Aland 28th
NA28.plain: Built from the earliest Greek copies we have. Used by most modern Bible translations.

NA27.name: Nestle-Aland 27th
NA27.plain: The previous version of the NA28. Nearly identical, with minor differences.

SBL.name: SBL Greek NT
SBL.plain: An edition by the Society of Biblical Literature, weighing the earliest copies.

WH.name: Westcott-Hort
WH.plain: Published in 1881. Gave heavy weight to two of the oldest surviving copies.

Treg.name: Tregelles
Treg.plain: Published in 1879 by Samuel Tregelles, who compared hundreds of early copies by hand.

Tyn.name: Tyndale House
Tyn.plain: Published in 2017 from Cambridge. Uses the single earliest copy available for each book.

TR.name: Textus Receptus
TR.plain: The Greek text used to translate the King James Bible (1611). Based on copies available in the 1500s.

Byz.name: Byzantine Text
Byz.plain: Based on the largest number of surviving copies, most from the 800s–1400s AD.

disputed.note: Not in the earliest manuscripts. Some scholars consider these verses a later addition.
Not in the earliest manuscripts. This passage about the woman caught in adultery may have been added later.
